episodes.title: Today: Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran, and DOJ releases new Epstein files
episodes.description:
Today as it Happened on Friday, March 6, 2026:

Iran escalated its assault on Israel overnight, the Israel Defense Forces said.

In a neighborhood north of Tel Aviv, an apartment building was struck by debris from a cluster munition that fell after Israeli forces intercepted an Iranian missile. The impact caused damage to the building, but no injuries were reported.

The attack underscores that despite large-scale strikes by the United States and Israel on Iranian targets, Tehran still retains the capability to launch missiles that can cause damage.

The conflict has expanded across several fronts. One of the U.S. military’s goals has been to cripple Iran’s naval forces. U.S. officials say American forces have destroyed about 30 Iranian naval vessels since the fighting began.

The Justice Department on Thursday released additional Jeffrey Epstein files involving uncorroborated accusations made by a woman against President Donald Trump that the department said had been mistakenly withheld during an earlier review.

The department said last week that it was working to determine if any records were improperly withheld after several news organizations reported that the massive tranche of records that had been made public didn’t include some files documenting a series of interviews conducted in 2019 with a woman who made an allegation against Trump.

The accuser was interviewed by the FBI four times as it sought to assess her account but a summary of only one of those interviews had been included in the publicly released files.

And thousands of people gathered at a church on Chicago's South Side to pay a final public tribute to the late Rev. Jesse Jackson Sr.

Barack Obama said Jackson's presidential runs in the 1980s set the stage for other Black leaders, including his own successful 2009 presidency and re-election.

“The message he sent to a 22-year-old child of a single mother with a funny name, an outsider, was that maybe there wasn’t any place or any room where we didn’t belong,” Obama said. “He paved the road for so many others to follow."


-------------------------------------

episodes.title: DOJ releases additional Epstein files with uncorroborated allegations against Trump
episodes.description:
The Justice Department has released additional documents related to the Jeffrey Epstein investigation, including records involving uncorroborated accusations against President Donald Trump.

Officials said the files had been mistakenly withheld during an earlier review because they were incorrectly coded as duplicates. The records stem from FBI interviews conducted in 2019 with a woman who made allegations involving Epstein and Trump.

The Justice Department noted the claims were uncorroborated and said some submissions to investigators included “untrue and sensationalist claims” about the president. Trump has also denied anywrong doing in relation to Epstein. 

Scripps News senior national correspondent Alex Miller reads what the woman alleged and a response from the White House.
-------------------------------------

episodes.title: Hillary Clinton Confronts Rep. Lauren Boebert Over Secret Photo During Epstein Deposition
episodes.description:
Watch the tense moment former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton and her legal team learned that Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colorado) had taken a photo of Clinton during a closed-door deposition and sent it to a right-wing commentator, who then posted it online.

The hearing was temporarily halted after Clinton, visibly frustrated, told lawmakers: "I am done with this if you guys are doing that, I am done. You can hold me in contempt from now until the cows come home."

Boebert later told reporters she took the picture because she “really admired her blue suit.”

Upon resuming, Clinton’s counsel argued this was exactly why they wanted the hearing to be public — to avoid disruptions and incidents like this.


-------------------------------------

episodes.title: Bill Clinton denies wrongdoing in Epstein deposition before House panel
episodes.description:
Former President Bill Clinton testified behind closed doors before the House Oversight Committee as lawmakers review matters related to Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell.

Clinton said he had no knowledge of Epstein’s crimes and ended his relationship with the financier before Epstein’s 2008 conviction. He also criticized the committee for subpoenaing former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.

A Republican on the panel said Clinton is being coopeartive and answering their questions.

episodes.title: Epstein survivors seek visibility, recognition from Trump at State of the Union
episodes.description:
More than a dozen survivors of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein will attend President Donald Trump's State of the Union address Tuesday night as guests of Democratic lawmakers.

For the survivors, their appearance is about visibility and ensuring the American people do not forget about the alleged crimes they endured. Weeks earlier, many of them attended a Capitol Hill hearing where Attorney General Pam Bondi largely deflected questions about the Justice Department's handling of the Epstein files. Some said they were re-traumatized by her testimony and feel there has been no accountability.

-------------------------------------

episodes.title: Epstein Survivors Plan to Attend President Trump's State of the Union Address
episodes.description:
Some of the survivors of abuse at the hands of convicted sex offenders Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell will attend President Donald Trump's State of the Union address on Tuesday night. 

A few of them joined members of the Democratic Women’s Caucus and House Democratic Caucus for a press conference earlier in the day.

episodes.title: New Mexico investigating claims of burials near Epstein's former ranch
episodes.description:
The New Mexico Department of Justice is investigating public land near what was once Jeffrey Epstein’s Zorro Ranch after a newly released email alleged that two foreign girls were buried there.

Last week, New Mexico Public Lands Commissioner Stephanie Garcia Richard said she requested the probe following a 2019 email from an alleged former Epstein staffer. The email claimed two girls were killed and buried on public land leased near the ranch, about 30 miles southeast of Santa Fe.

The redacted email was part of recently released documents from the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ investigation into Epstein. The deaths were allegedly at the direction of Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell, who is serving a 20-year federal prison sentence for sex trafficking.

-------------------------------------

episodes.title: Trump responds to Prince Andrew arrest over Epstein ties
episodes.description:
President Donald Trump took questions aboard Air Force One after reports that Prince Andrew was arrested in London in connection with Jeffrey Epstein. Trump said the situation is “very sad” for the royal family and emphasized that he has been “totally exonerated” in matters related to Epstein.

The president also addressed rising tensions with Iran, saying a deal must be reached within 10 to 15 days or it would be “unfortunate” for Tehran. 

In addition, Trump responded to comments from former President Barack Obama about aliens. He also briefly weighed in on questions surrounding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie.

Here’s what the president said during the exchange.

episodes.title: Did billionaire Les Wexner fund Epstein? Democrats hold deposition
episodes.description:
House Democrats questioned billionaire Leslie Wexner as part of their investigation into convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

Wexner, the former CEO of Victoria’s Secret’s parent company, sought to minimize his relationship with Epstein, Democrats said during a break in the deposition.

"We should be very clear that there would be no Epstein island, there would be no Epstein plane, there would be no money to traffic women and girls," Rep. Robert Garcia said. "Mr. Epstein would not be the wealthy man he was without the support of Les Wexner."

Democrats said they are aware of approximately $1 billion in Wexner’s wealth that was transferred to Epstein.

"It's important to know that Mr. Wexner appears to be unaware of much of the money that he claims that was given over to Mr. Epstein," Garcia said. "In fact, it's interesting that Mr. Wexner has already begun to clarify, in his mind, that somehow he and Mr. Epstein weren't even friends."

Garcia also said Wexner, 88, traveled on Epstein’s plane and visited his island.

-------------------------------------

episodes.title: Today: The harrowing diary of an Epstein victim and the money made from detaining immigrants
episodes.description:
Today as it Happened on Monday, February 16, 2026:

A diary written by a young girl details graphic accounts of abuse involving convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

The handwritten pages describe trauma she says she endured.

Dozens of diary pages that were made public in the release of Epstein-related investigative materials focus on what the girl describes as repeated abuse, including multiple pregnancies. She provides detailed accounts of childbirth with Ghislaine Maxwell allegedly in the room and of a baby, born alive, being taken away.

The first year of President Trump's push to arrest and deport undocumented immigrants was a financial boon for two companies that own and operate private detention centers.

CoreCivic and The GEO Group both reported their year-end earnings for 2025.

Each company reported seeing a boost in revenue of more than 13 percent, both making more than $2 billion.

GEO Group's executive chairman George Zoley called 2025 the "most successful year for new business wins in our company's history."

The two companies opened nine new detention centers across the country under contracts with ICE, adding thousands more beds able to hold arrested immigrants who are awaiting deportation hearings.

And the FBI late last week told Minnesota investigators it would not share any information or evidence related to the shooting death of Alex Pretti, who died in Minneapolis in January.

-------------------------------------

episodes.title: 'I’m beyond broken': Diary in Epstein files details abuse girl says she endured
episodes.description:
Newly released pages from the Epstein files include a diary written by a young girl describing repeated abuse, pregnancies and trauma she says she endured while around Jeffrey Epstein.

The handwritten entries, which include poems, sonograms and graduation mementos, offer a disturbing look at what she describes as years of suffering.

Scripps News examines the diary pages and the broader questions they raise about investigations tied to the Epstein case.

episodes.title: Today: Were girls buried by Jeffrey Epstein’s Zorro Ranch?
episodes.description:
A top New Mexico official is calling for an investigation into public land near Jeffrey Epstein’s Zorro Ranch after a newly released email alleged that two foreign girls were buried there.

Land Commissioner Stephanie Garcia Richard said she requested an investigation after learning about a 2019 email included in recently released Epstein-related documents. The email, sent to a conservative radio host, claimed that two girls were buried on public land leased near the ranch. The act was allegedly done at the direction of Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell, who is serving a 20-year federal prison sentence for sex trafficking.

“There was a very disturbing allegation that came out that could potentially be linked to state land,” Garcia Richard said. “Because I am the manager of that land. I’m the elected steward of that land and what occurs there and what the land is used for is of utmost interest and importance to us at the State Land Office.”

episodes.title: Bondi Refuses to Apologize to Epstein Survivors in Heated Hearing on Capitol Hill
episodes.description:
During a tense House Judiciary Committee hearing, Rep. Pramila Jayapal (D-Wash.) demanded that Attorney General Pam Bondi apologize to survivors of Jeffrey Epstein present in the audience. 

Bondi said, "I'm not going to get in the gutter for her theatrics."

Jayapal also asked the survivors to raise their hands if they had not met with the Department of Justice—every single one did. 
-------------------------------------

episodes.title: Ghislaine Maxwell pleads the Fifth, seeks clemency from President Trump
episodes.description:
Ghislaine Maxwell refused to answer questions during a deposition before the House Oversight Committee, invoking her Fifth Amendment right amid an ongoing congressional investigation tied to Jeffrey Epstein.

Maxwell’s attorney said she is remaining silent due to a pending habeas petition, adding that she would be willing to testify if granted clemency by President Donald Trump. Maxwell is currently serving a 20-year federal prison sentence following her 2021 sex trafficking conviction.

Committee leaders say the investigation will continue without Maxwell’s testimony, with several individuals linked to Epstein expected to appear in the weeks ahead.

-------------------------------------

episodes.title: Epstein survivor says she thinks ICE actions are to steer people away from release of all the files
episodes.description:
Lisa Phillips, a survivor of Jeffrey Epstein's crimes and advocate for other survivors, said she is losing faith in the justice system since the Epstein files have still not been released in their entirety -- and there's no update in sight. 

"They've never been worried about the victims," said Phillips. She said the stuff they have released feels like a joke.

episodes.title: Comer threatens contempt as Clintons reject subpoenas in Epstein investigation
episodes.description:
House Oversight Chairman James Comer responds after former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton declined to appear for testimony requested by the committee.

The Clintons’ attorneys argue the subpoenas tied to the Jeffrey Epstein investigation are invalid and legally unenforceable, saying they have already provided all relevant information in writing. Comer has threatened potential contempt proceedings, setting up a possible legal confrontation between Congress and the former officeholders.

In this video, Comer addresses the committee’s next steps and responds to the Clintons’ refusal to testify.

episodes.title: Epstein survivors demand answers after DOJ misses full file release deadline
episodes.description:
After the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) failed to fully comply with a law to release all files related to its investigation into convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, a group of his survivors on Monday accused the agency of legal violations and are demanding accountability.

The DOJ faced a Friday deadline to release its massive cache of records related to the federal investigation into Epstein and his associates. However, the release sparked widespread outrage after only a portion of the documents were made public — many of which were heavily redacted.

A group of 19 women, including two who chose to remain anonymous, said Monday that there is no excuse for the delay.

"The public received a fraction of the files, and what we received was riddled with abnormal and extreme redactions with no explanation," the group said in a statement obtained by Scripps News. "At the same time, numerous victim identities were left unredacted, causing real and immediate harm. No financial documents were released. Grand jury minutes, though approved by a federal judge for release, were fully blacked out – not the scattered redactions that might be expected to protect victim names, but 119 full pages blacked out. We are told that there are hundreds of thousands of pages of documents still unreleased. These are clear-cut violations of an unambiguous law."

-------------------------------------

episodes.title: A victim reacts to the newly released Epstein investigation files
episodes.description:
Files from the federal investigation into sex offender Jeffrey Epstein released Friday include photos, court documents, taped interviews and other materials.

The hundreds of thousands of documents released on Friday show many of Epstein's victims, whose images and other details have been heavily redacted for their protection. Some documents show hand-written testimony describing victims' interaction with Epstein.

While the DOJ has not yet released all of the investigation files it is legally required to, at least one victim says she is vindicated.

Maria Farmer reported Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell's activities to the FBI in 1996 — a fact that Friday's release confirmed.

Farmer issued a statement following the release of the files:

"I'm crying for two reasons: I want everyone to know that I'm shedding tears of joy for myself, but also tears of sorrow for all of the other victims that the FBI failed."

-------------------------------------
